name: Gnocchi Alla Giordano
recipeIngredient: 1 Whole egg plus 1 Egg yolk, lightly beaten
recipeInstructions:
2 tablespoons Unsalted butter -- softened
1 teaspoon Salt
Freshly grated Parmesan cheese
Tomato sauce
(Anna Teresa Callan's "Menus for Pasta")
Boil the potatoes in their jackets. Drain them and peel. Put through a ricer or food mill.
While the potatoes are still warm, blend in the flour. Add the egg, egg yolk, butter and salt.
Place the potato mixture on a floured board. Knead lightly. The dough will be soft.
Roll the dough into 1-inch thick sticks about 10 inches long.
Cut each roll into 3/4-inch pieces.
Rub each piece of dough lightly over the coarse side of a cheese grater.
In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook the gnocchi until they rise to the top of the water. Using a slotted spoon, remove the gnocchi to a warm bowl. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, top with tomato sauce and serve at once.
